resources.string: Explore the greatest eras and legends

The pioneering era where legends were born. Front-engine cars, minimal safety, and pure racing spirit defined this golden age. Drivers raced on public roads and circuits with no barriers, no fire suits, and no run-off areas — pure bravery was the only safety equipment.
Juan Manuel Fangio · Jim Clark · Jackie Stewart

Revolutionary turbo engines brought unprecedented power — some qualifying units produced over 1,400 horsepower. Ground effect aerodynamics changed racing forever, and the era was defined by fierce rivalries, political battles between teams, and some of the most dramatic championship fights in history.
Niki Lauda · Alain Prost · Ayrton Senna

Hybrid power units and advanced aerodynamics define the modern era. Data-driven racing with unprecedented technology, global reach across 24 races, and a new generation of fans drawn in by Drive to Survive. The sport has never been more popular or more technically complex.
Michael Schumacher · Lewis Hamilton · Max Verstappen
Tap any circuit to reveal the track map

Ayrton Senna is widely regarded as the greatest Formula 1 driver of all time. His supernatural ability in wet conditions, combined with an almost spiritual connection to his car, produced performances that transcended sport. Three world championships with McLaren, 65 pole positions, and a qualifying lap at Monaco 1984 that left rivals speechless. His rivalry with Alain Prost defined an era. Senna\'s tragic death at Imola in 1994 left a void in motorsport that has never truly been filled.
“If you no longer go for a gap that exists, you are no longer a racing driver.”

Michael Schumacher redefined what it meant to be a Formula 1 champion. His five consecutive titles with Ferrari from 2000–2004 remain one of sport\'s greatest dynasties. Schumacher\'s relentless work ethic, technical feedback, and physical conditioning set new standards for the sport. He returned with Mercedes in 2010, proving his dedication to racing. His 91 race wins stood as the all-time record for 16 years.
“Once something is a passion, the motivation is there.”

Lewis Hamilton is statistically the greatest Formula 1 driver in history. Seven world championships, 104 pole positions, 105 race wins — every major record bears his name. His 2007 debut season nearly ended in a title, and he has never stopped pushing boundaries. Beyond the track, Hamilton has used his platform to champion diversity and social justice, becoming one of sport\'s most influential figures. Now racing for Ferrari, his story continues.

Niki Lauda\'s story is one of the most extraordinary in sport. After a near-fatal crash at the 1976 German Grand Prix left him with severe burns, he returned to racing just 42 days later — still bleeding beneath his helmet. He lost that year\'s title to James Hunt by a single point, but came back to win two more championships. His analytical mind and brutal honesty made him as influential off the track as on it. A true legend of courage and intellect.
“I was never afraid of the car. I was afraid of my own mistakes.”

Max Verstappen became the youngest driver to start an F1 race at just 17 years old, and has since rewritten the record books. Four consecutive world championships from 2021–2024 with Red Bull Racing, including a record-breaking 2023 season where he won 19 of 22 races. His aggressive, fearless driving style and ice-cold racecraft under pressure have drawn comparisons to the all-time greats. Still only in his mid-twenties, the question is no longer whether he will be remembered as one of the best — but how many more titles he will win.
“I don\'t think about records. I just want to win every race I enter.”
🏁 Became F1\'s youngest ever race winner at 18 years, 228 days (Spain 2016)
⚡ Won 19 out of 22 races in 2023 — the most dominant season in F1 history
🎮 An avid sim racer — he competes in real endurance events like the Daytona 24H
🇳🇱 His home race at Zandvoort sells out in minutes — the Dutch GP effect is real
🔢 Chose the number 33 throughout his career before switching to #1 as champion
Sign in to continue your F1 journey
Create your account to get started
New to F1, learning the basics
Deep knowledge of teams and strategies
